Built for Cris · UA Student Recreation Center

5 days/week · low-impact lower body

Plan Calendar Equipment Warm-up Mon Tue Wed Thu Fri Red flags
Read this first. I'm not a doctor or physical therapist. Given your tibia/fibula history, please run this plan past your doctor or a PT before starting. This plan is conservative on purpose — machines over free weights, no high impact, careful with the leg.

Weekly schedule

DayFocus

Equipment reference

Stock photos to help you spot the equipment. Brand/color at UA will differ but the shape is the same.

Not pictured: Glute Drive (hip thrust) and Belt Squat machines. Ask a staff member to point them out — both are distinctive.

Warm-up (10 min, every session)

  1. 5 min recumbent bike, easy.
  2. Ankle circles: 10 each direction, each foot.
  3. Wall ankle stretch: 10 reps each side.
  4. Cat-cow: 10 reps.
  5. Glute bridge: 10 reps.
  6. Arm circles: 10 forward, 10 back.
  7. Bodyweight squat to bench: 10 reps.

Day 1 — Monday

Upper Body Push · 45 to 60 min
Rest between sets: 60 to 90 sec between sets.

Chest Press Machine

3 × 10
  • Seat so the handles line up with the middle of your chest.
  • Feet flat, back and head on the pad.
  • Push smoothly, stop just before locking out. Lower over 2 sec.
  • Felt in shoulders more than chest? Lower the seat a notch.
Sets done:

Seated Shoulder Press

3 × 10
  • Handles at about ear level when you sit down.
  • Press straight up, don't lock the elbows.
  • Keep lower back pressed gently into the pad. If you have to arch hard, go lighter.
Sets done:

Lat Pulldown — narrow grip (optional warm-up)

2 × 12
  • Use the V-shaped close-grip handle.
  • Pull to upper chest, squeeze, return all the way up.
Sets done:

Cable Tricep Pushdown (rope)

3 × 12
  • Attach rope to the high pulley on a cable station.
  • Elbows pinned at sides. Only the forearms move.
  • Press down, pull rope ends slightly apart at the bottom.
Sets done:

Dumbbell Lateral Raise

3 × 12
  • Start light — 5 to 10 lb dumbbells.
  • Raise out to the sides until arms are at shoulder height (not higher).
  • Lead with the elbows, slight bend. Lower slow.
Sets done:

Pec Deck (Chest Fly Machine)

2 × 12
  • Seat so handles are at chest level.
  • Bring handles together in front of chest, slight elbow bend.
  • Squeeze 1 sec, return slow. Don't let the stack slam.
Sets done:

Front Plank

3 × 30 sec
  • Forearms on floor, elbows under shoulders, body straight.
  • Squeeze glutes, brace stomach.
  • Stop the set when hips start to sag.
Sets done:

Day 2 — Tuesday

Lower Body (modified, low-impact) · 45 to 60 min
Rest between sets: 90 to 120 sec for big moves (belt squat, leg press, glute drive). 60 sec for the rest.

Belt Squat (Resistance Training Room)

3 × 8–10
  • Best lower-body option for your leg — weight loads through the hip belt, not the spine.
  • If busy, swap in Leg Press at lighter weight with shorter range of motion.
  • First session: 2 sets with just the bar/belt, no added plates.
  • Stand upright, push the floor away, go only as deep as feels controlled and pain-free.
Sets done:

Leg Press Machine

3 × 10
  • Whole back on the pad. Feet shoulder-width, mid-foot on the platform.
  • Lower only as far as you can without pain or lower back rounding off.
  • Push through mid-foot, don't lock the knees.
  • If old injury hurts, raise foot position higher on the platform.
Sets done:

Glute Drive Machine (hip thrust)

3 × 10
  • Cushion across hips, upper back on the back pad.
  • Push through heels, drive hips up to form a flat line from shoulders to knees.
  • Squeeze glutes 1 full second at the top. Lower with control.
  • Strong glutes take stress off the lower leg in everyday walking.
Sets done:

Seated Leg Curl Machine

3 × 12
  • Pad sits just above the back of your ankles.
  • Curl heels under the seat. Pause 1 sec, return slow.
  • Strong hamstrings protect the knee.
Sets done:

Seated Calf Raise Machine

3 × 15
  • Most important move for you in the whole plan.
  • Balls of feet on the platform. Lower heels as far as comfortable, rise as high as you can.
  • Pause 1 sec at top. Slow on the way down.
Sets done:

Hip Abductor Machine

2 × 15
  • Pads against the outsides of your knees.
  • Push knees out, pause, return slow.
  • Strengthens hip stabilizers — often weak after favoring a leg.
Sets done:

Recumbent Bike — easy spin

10 min
  • Cool down. Easy pace — you should be able to hold a conversation.
  • Recumbent (not upright) keeps weight off the lower leg.
Sets done:

Day 3 — Wednesday

Upper Body Pull · 45 to 60 min
Rest between sets: 60 to 90 sec.

Lat Pulldown Machine

3 × 10
  • Thigh pad snug. Wide overhand grip, hands a bit wider than shoulders.
  • Lean back ~15°. Pull bar to upper chest.
  • Drive elbows down and back. Let the bar return all the way up.
Sets done:

Seated Cable Row

3 × 10
  • V-handle. Feet on the foot plate, slight knee bend.
  • Sit tall. Pull handle to your stomach, squeeze shoulder blades.
  • No yanking with the lower back.
Sets done:

Chest-Supported Row or Hammer Strength Row

3 × 10
  • Face-down on the angled pad. Palms facing each other.
  • Pull handles toward hips, squeeze back. Return slow.
  • The chest pad takes the lower back out of the equation.
Sets done:

Cable Face Pull

3 × 15
  • Attach rope just above head height.
  • Pull rope toward forehead — hands end by ears, elbows high.
  • Shoulder-health and posture move. Light weight, don't ego-lift.
Sets done:

Dumbbell Bicep Curl

3 × 12
  • Stand tall, dumbbells at sides, palms forward.
  • Curl up to shoulder height, no swinging.
  • Lower over 2 sec. The slow lower builds the muscle.
Sets done:

Dead Bug

3 × 8 per side
  • On back, arms straight up, knees bent 90° over hips.
  • Lower opposite arm + leg toward floor without lower back lifting.
  • Slow and controlled.
Sets done:

Recumbent Bike — easy spin

10 min
  • Same as Tuesday — easy cool-down.
Sets done:

Day 4 — Thursday

Recovery + Mobility · ~45 min
Rest between sets: —

Pick one: Recumbent Bike, Elliptical, Rower, or Pool

25–30 min
  • Easy to moderate pace. Conversation in short sentences.
  • Treadmill walking is fine if the leg feels good. Skip if any pain.
  • Pool is great once a week — even just walking laps in waist-deep water.
Sets done:

Ankle Mobility Routine

1 round
  • Ankle circles: 10 each direction, each foot.
  • Wall ankle stretch: toes a few inches from wall, drive knee to touch wall without heel lifting. 10 reps each side.
  • Heel walks across room, then toe walks back.
  • Calf stretch on wall: straight-leg 30 sec, then bent-knee 30 sec, each side.
Sets done:

Hip Mobility Routine

1 round
  • Half-kneeling hip flexor stretch: 30 sec each side.
  • 90/90 hip stretch: both legs bent 90°, one front, one side. Lean over front leg. 30 sec each side.
  • Glute bridge: 2 × 12.
  • World's greatest stretch: from lunge, hand to floor inside front foot, other hand up. 5 each side.
Sets done:

Core Circuit

2 rounds
  • Bird dog: 8 per side. Hands and knees, extend opposite arm + leg, hold a beat.
  • Side plank: 20 sec per side. Drop to knees if needed.
  • Pallof press at cable: 8 per side. Stand sideways to cable at chest height, press straight out and back, resist rotation.
Sets done:

Day 5 — Friday

Full Body Strength · 30 to 45 min
Rest between sets: 60 to 90 sec.

Goblet Squat to Bench

3 × 8
  • Hold one dumbbell vertically against chest. Bench behind you.
  • Sit back and down, tap the bench, stand back up.
  • Bench is your depth target and safety net. Start 15–25 lb.
  • If this hurts the leg: skip and do Leg Press at light weight instead.
Sets done:

Dumbbell Bench Press

3 × 10
  • Flat bench, one DB each hand at chest level, palms forward.
  • Press straight up over chest, stop just before they touch.
  • Lower under control to a gentle stretch.
  • Sit up, DBs on thighs, then back on rack — never drop them.
Sets done:

Machine Row or Cable Row

3 × 10
  • Pick whichever machine is free.
  • Squeeze shoulder blades together at the end of each rep.
Sets done:

Standing Calf Raise

3 × 15
  • Balls of feet on a platform or step.
  • Rise high, pause 1 sec, lower under control.
  • Add weight only when you can do 15 clean reps bodyweight.
Sets done:

Farmer's Carry

3 × 30 sec
  • Pick up a moderate DB in each hand. Stand tall, shoulders back.
  • Walk in a straight line 30 sec. Turn and walk back.
  • Best carry-over-to-real-life move in the plan. Start 20–25 lb.
Sets done:

Pallof Press (cable)

2 × 10 per side
  • Stand sideways to cable at chest height. Handle at your chest, both hands.
  • Press handle straight out in front. Resist the cable's rotation.
  • Hold 2 sec, return. Slow and controlled.
Sets done:

When to stop

Not normal — stop and contact your doctor/PT:

  • Sharp pain in the old fracture site.
  • Swelling in the leg that doesn't go down in a few hours.
  • Numbness, tingling, or pins-and-needles in foot or lower leg.
  • Pain that gets worse hours after the workout.
  • Popping, snapping, or "giving way" feeling.

Normal: muscle soreness 24–48 hours after a workout, mild fatigue, breathlessness during sets.

Progression

When you can finish all sets at the top of the rep range with ~2 reps left in the tank, add the smallest available weight increment next session.

If new weight feels too hard, drop back one session and try again the next.

First 2 weeks: don't add weight. Learn the movements first.
